name: Buddy DeFranco
abstract: Boniface Ferdinand Leonard "Buddy" DeFranco (February 17, 1923 – December 24, 2014) was an American jazz clarinetist. In addition to his work as a bandleader, DeFranco led the Glenn Miller Orchestra for almost a decade in the 1960s and 1970s.

interviews.title: Buddy DeFranco interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 13, 1996
DeFranco Buddy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Jazz at the Philharmonic
JVC Jazz Festival: Mel Tormé and Nancy Wilson, Singers
JVC Jazz Festival: The Legacy of Woody Herman

about_quotes.speaker: Eiji Kitamura
about_quotes.evidence: I played many, many times double clarinet with Buddy DeFranco, in the tour in Japan, Australia, and ’96 we went to the Paris and I played double clarinet with Buddy DeFranco.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eiji Kitamura interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: I work as much as can with Buddy DeFranco. That’s my favorite of all. We’ve been playing together for 25 years as co-leaders
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Hardaway
about_quotes.evidence: Yes I did. Well I did an album with him... it was Jimmy Rowles was on that... and Buddy DeFranco and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Hardaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: In the front line was me, Buddy DeFranco, and a saxophone player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Anat Cohen
about_quotes.evidence: including Buddy DeFranco and Kenny Davern... I just went on stage and I went to the clarinet and I went to the front and I played two choruses of rhythm changes and then the minute I finished I had two hands grab me by the shoulder and led me back and it was Kenny Davern who held me and took me back
about_quotes.doc_title: Anat Cohen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 9, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: He says, “I worked with them at the Brass Rail when he had Clark Terry, Wardell Gray, Buddy DeFranco, and in the rhythm section Freddie Green, Jimmy Lewis and Gus Johnson.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Lewis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995
